summary: Comprehensive backend testing (21 pytest cases) and frontend smoke testing for Sistema POS Ecuador. 20/21 backend tests passed (95%). Frontend login + redirect-to-setup flow verified; setup wizard loads lookup data correctly.

critical.issue: Returns 500 - 'AttributeError: bytearray object has no attribute encode'. fpdf2's pdf.output() returns bytearray; FastAPIResponse content treats it as str. Fix: wrap with bytes(pdf.output()) in routes/invoice_routes.py line 195

minor.issue: server.py: CORS_ORIGINS=* combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec. Browsers reject Access-Control-Allow-Origin:* with credentials. Currently works only because frontend & backend share the same origin via ingress, but will break if origin differs. Recommend setting CORS_ORIGINS explicitly to FRONTEND_URL.
Lines 113-114 have dead code: 'cedula_base = ruc[:10]' is assigned then immediately returns False. Logic is still correct (rejects non-001 endings) but the line is unreachable/misleading.
Soft-deletes (is_active=False) but GET /api/inventory/products/{id} still returns the product (no is_active filter). Inconsistent: list endpoint filters by is_active but single-get does not.
Comparison `current_user['_id'] == user_id` compares ObjectId str with raw user_id string; may always be False unless types align. Self-deactivation guard could be unreliable.

issues: Login card is centered but lots of empty space on desktop; consider a 2-column hero layout.
Card appears very small at top of large viewports; no max content density; could feel sparse.

action_items: Fix PDF download 500: change `return pdf.output()` consumer to `bytes(pdf.output())` or use `pdf.output(dest='S').encode('latin-1')` in invoice_routes.generate_ride_pdf / download_pdf
Tighten CORS: do not combine allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=['*']
Either filter is_active in GET /api/inventory/products/{id} or hard-delete; align with list endpoint
Fix self-deactivation guard in user_routes.deactivate_user (compare str(user['_id']) to user_id)
critical_code_review_comments: PDF: fpdf2 returns bytearray; Starlette Response with bytearray content triggers 500 (well-known issue).
JWT secret has a safe default fallback only in CORS_ORIGINS; JWT_SECRET correctly required via os.environ[].
Multi-tenant data isolation looks correct: all queries scope by business_id.
Brute-force lockout implemented (5 attempts / 15 min) and observed working.
httpOnly cookies set on login as expected.
test_credentials: admin@sistema.com / Admin123! (superadmin, no business_id). Tests dynamically create TEST_owner_<random>@example.com for tenant flows.
seed_data_creation: Tests create a TEST business (RUC 1714616123001), TEST products, TEST sales, TEST invoices, TEST users prefixed with TEST_ in DB. Re-running tests will skip business setup as RUC is already used (handled via pytest.skip in fixture).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_pos_ecuador.py use shared module-scoped fixture `tenant_context` that creates one business via /api/business/setup with RUC 1714616123001. On 2nd run it will skip downstream tests (duplicate RUC). To re-run cleanly, drop the test DB or change VALID_RUC. PDF endpoint failing is the main backend bug. Frontend was only smoke-tested for login->setup redirect; full POS/Inventory/Invoices UI flows still need playwright coverage after main agent fixes PDF.
rca of the issue: PDF 500: fpdf2's PDF.output() (when called without dest argument) returns a bytearray. Starlette's Response.render does `content.encode(self.charset)` if content is not bytes — bytearray has no .encode method → AttributeError → 500. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/login as tenant, GET /api/invoices/{id}/pdf. Fix: in invoice_routes.py line 195, change `pdf_bytes = generate_ride_pdf(invoice)` consumer to `return FastAPIResponse(content=bytes(pdf_bytes), ...)` OR change generate_ride_pdf return to `bytes(pdf.output())`.
